name: Databricks
tagline: SAS wins on governance, industry solutions, and production-grade decisioning

overview.whatTheyDo: Unified data and AI platform built on Apache Spark. Combines data engineering (Delta Lake, ETL pipelines) with ML experimentation (MLflow, Mosaic AI) in a lakehouse architecture.
overview.sweetSpot: Data engineering teams wanting one platform for ETL + ML. Cloud-native, Python-first organizations with strong engineering cultures.
overview.typicalBuyer: CDO, Head of Data Engineering, VP Data Platform
overview.phPresence: No direct PH office (Singapore hub). Sold through Azure/AWS marketplaces and local resellers. Growing in PH tech companies but limited banking references.
overview.recentMomentum: Mosaic AI (GenAI), Unity Catalog open-sourced, Tabular (Iceberg) acquisition, pre-IPO at ~$60B valuation.
overview.pricingModel: Consumption-based (DBU per hour). Appears transparent but costs spike at scale. No bundled governance — each add-on increases cost.

whereWeWin.headline: Governed Model Lifecycle (BSP 1112 Compliant)
whereWeWin.explanation: SAS Model Manager provides end-to-end model governance with approval workflows, documentation generation, and regulatory audit trails — specifically designed for BSP Circular 1112 model risk management requirements. Databricks MLflow tracks experiments but lacks formal approval chains and regulatory documentation.
whereWeWin.proofPoint: 9 of top 10 global banks use SAS for model governance.

whereWeWin.headline: Real-Time Fraud Decisioning (AFASA Ready)
whereWeWin.explanation: SAS Intelligent Decisioning combines business rules + ML models + network analytics for real-time scoring in <10ms. Meets BSP Circular 1213 FMS requirements out-of-the-box. Databricks requires custom engineering for equivalent real-time decisioning.
whereWeWin.proofPoint: SAS processes billions of fraud transactions daily across 3,000+ financial institutions.

whereWeWin.headline: Business User Self-Service (No Code)
whereWeWin.explanation: SAS Visual Analytics + Model Studio enable business analysts and citizen data scientists to build, validate, and deploy models without writing Python. Databricks AI/BI (Genie) is emerging but narrow compared to SAS's mature visual interfaces.
whereWeWin.proofPoint: 60% of SAS Viya users are non-programmers who contribute to production analytics.

whereWeWin.headline: Pre-Built Industry Solutions
whereWeWin.explanation: SAS delivers production-ready solutions for fraud, AML, risk, clinical trials, and supply chain — with domain expertise encoded from 50 years of industry engagement. Databricks is a horizontal toolkit where everything must be built from scratch.
whereWeWin.proofPoint: SAS Fraud Management deployed at 200+ financial institutions globally, with pre-built detection models for Philippine payment fraud patterns.

whereTheyWin.whyItMatters: Spark-native processing, Delta Lake, Unity Catalog provide a strong foundation for data teams already using Databricks for ETL. Hard to dislodge once embedded.
whereTheyWin.howToNeutralize: Position SAS as the 'last mile' — Databricks for data engineering, SAS for governed analytics and production decisioning. Better Together architecture.

whereTheyWin.whyItMatters: Notebook-driven, Git-integrated, Python/SQL-first experience appeals to modern data science teams. Young data scientists learn Databricks in bootcamps.
whereTheyWin.howToNeutralize: SAS Viya supports Python and R natively. Show SAS Studio's modern interface. Emphasize that governance and production readiness matter more than notebook convenience.

whereTheyWin.whyItMatters: Mosaic AI, DBRX foundation models, model serving for LLMs — Databricks is investing heavily in GenAI infrastructure.
whereTheyWin.howToNeutralize: SAS adds governance FOR GenAI — prompt monitoring, output quality gates, model cards for LLMs. Position: 'Build on Databricks, govern with SAS.'

whereTheyWin.whyItMatters: Large community, extensive integrations, partner ecosystem. Customers feel safe choosing a platform with broad adoption.
whereTheyWin.howToNeutralize: SAS has 50 years of enterprise relationships. In regulated industries, proven track record > community size. Ask: 'Will your regulator accept a Databricks notebook as model documentation?'

features.notes: Databricks Unity Catalog improving but lacks BSP Circular 1112 depth
Databricks has A/B via MLflow but lacks formal approval workflows and regulatory audit trails
Purpose-built for regulated industries; BSP 1213 FMS-ready
Databricks AI/BI (Genie) emerging but narrow vs. SAS VA + Model Studio
SAS Intelligent Decisioning vs. custom MLflow setup
Fraud, AML, risk, clinical, supply chain pre-built
Both strong; SAS adds governance layer to AutoML output
SAS has native interpretability across all algorithms
SAS runs anywhere; Databricks primarily cloud (limited on-prem emerging)
Databricks has strong GenAI/LLM via Mosaic AI; SAS has purpose-built NLP for structured text
Native in SAS Model Manager vs. MLflow add-ons
SAS has dedicated enterprise forecasting suite
SAS Data Quality embedded in pipeline
Databricks leads on GenAI infra; SAS adds governance layer for LLM outputs

landmines.question: How do you demonstrate model lineage and governance to BSP examiners during on-site examination?
landmines.expectedAnswer: They struggle or say 'we use notebooks' — not acceptable for BSP
landmines.whatToSayNext: SAS Model Manager auto-generates regulatory documentation. Let me show you what a BSP-ready model report looks like.

landmines.question: What's your process for promoting a model from development to production with formal approval workflows?
landmines.expectedAnswer: If informal or engineering-driven — no regulated governance
landmines.whatToSayNext: BSP Circular 1112 requires documented approval chains. SAS has built-in workflow with role-based approvals.

landmines.question: How do your business analysts participate in the analytics process today?
landmines.expectedAnswer: They don't — it's all Python engineers
landmines.whatToSayNext: That's common. SAS enables analysts to build and validate models visually, expanding your analytics capacity without hiring more engineers.

landmines.question: Can you show me your real-time fraud scoring architecture — what latency do you achieve?
landmines.expectedAnswer: Batch or slow (>100ms) — not meeting BSP 1213 FMS requirements
landmines.whatToSayNext: SAS scores in <10ms with network analytics. That's what BSP Circular 1213 requires for real-time behavioral monitoring.

landmines.question: What happens to your analytics if you need to move to a different cloud provider?
landmines.expectedAnswer: Significant rework — Databricks notebooks have cloud dependencies
landmines.whatToSayNext: SAS runs identically on AWS, Azure, GCP, or on-prem. Zero code changes between environments.

objections.objection: Databricks can do everything SAS does
objections.whyTheySayIt: Databricks sales positions as full platform. Customer hasn't tested governance depth.
objections.response: Databricks excels at data engineering and experimentation. But ask them to show: model approval workflows, regulatory audit trails, champion/challenger with automatic rollback, and real-time decisioning combining business rules with ML. These are native in SAS — they require custom engineering on Databricks.
proofPoints: 9/10 top banks use SAS for governance
BSP examiners specifically look for documented model lifecycle
objections.whatNotToSay: Don't say 'Databricks is just for data engineering' — that's dismissive and inaccurate.

objections.whyTheySayIt: Perception from SAS 9.4 era. Haven't seen Viya.
objections.response: SAS Viya runs on Kubernetes, supports Python/R natively, has modern REST APIs, and deploys on any cloud. The 50 years isn't baggage — it's 50 years of statistical algorithms and production hardening. Show them: Viya on AKS, Python code running in SAS Studio, the CI/CD pipeline.
proofPoints: Runs on same K8s infrastructure as Databricks
objections.whatNotToSay: Don't get defensive. Acknowledge SAS 9.4 was old. Pivot to Viya immediately.

objections.whyTheySayIt: Lower sticker price per DBU. Customer hasn't done full TCO.
objections.response: Compare total cost: Databricks DBUs + cloud compute (spikes with auto-scaling) + hiring Spark engineers + building governance from scratch + building monitoring + compliance consulting. SAS includes governance, monitoring, industry solutions, and support. The delivered cost is often 30-40% lower than build-from-scratch.
proofPoints: Gartner TCO analysis favors integrated platforms for regulated workloads
PH market: hard to hire Spark/MLflow engineers locally
objections.whatNotToSay: Don't attack Databricks pricing directly — instead, broaden the comparison to TCO.

phContext.localPresence: No direct Philippines office. Nearest team is Singapore. Sold through Azure Marketplace and AWS Marketplace. Some local SI partners but no dedicated PH account team.
knownCustomers: Growing adoption in PH tech companies
Some pilot activity in progressive banks via Azure deals
phContext.localPricing: Consumption-based DBUs — attractive for small workloads but unpredictable at scale. No PH-specific pricing. USD-denominated.
regulatoryGaps: No native BSP Circular 1213 FMS compliance
Model documentation doesn't meet BSP 1112 examination format
No AML/CFT-specific solution — must build from scratch

partnerWhen: Customer has >$500K/yr Databricks investment for data engineering
Data engineering team is happy — gap is governance/production ML
Regulatory pressure creates need for SAS Model Manager on top
competeWhen: Customer evaluating Databricks for first time
Primary use case is analytics/ML (not data engineering)
Databricks is pitching Unity Catalog as 'enough' for governance
